---
title: "sign_kalshi_request"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `sign_kalshi_request()`

Internal RSA-PSS Signing via OpenSSL System Call

## Usage

```r
sign_kalshi_request(timestamp, method, path)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `timestamp` | Character. A millisecond Unix timestamp string. |
| `method` | Character. The HTTP method in uppercase (e.g., "GET", "POST"). |
| `path` | Character. The API endpoint path without query parameters. |

## Value

A base64-encoded string containing the RSA-PSS signature.

## Description

Performs RSA-PSS signing using the system's 'openssl' binary to generate
the 'KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE' header.

## Details

The R 'openssl' package does not expose RSA-PSS padding, so this function
interfaces directly with the system's 'openssl' installation.

Signature is generated using:
- Message: 'timestamp + method + path'
- Algorithm: SHA-256
- Padding: RSA-PSS
- Salt Length: Equal to digest length (32 bytes)
